heading: National registry of substantiated cases of child abuse
label: 20990 National registry of substantiated cases of child abuse
notes: References in Text, referred to in subsecs. (c)(2)(B) and (f), was redesignated by , , .Section 5106a(b)(2)(A) of title 42section 5106a(b)(2)(B) of title 42Pub. L. 111–320, title I, § 115(c)(2)(A)124 Stat. 3469Dec. 20, 2010
CodificationSection was formerly classified to , The Public Health and Welfare, prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.section 16990 of Title 42
amendments: References in Text, referred to in subsecs. (c)(2)(B) and (f), was redesignated by , , .Section 5106a(b)(2)(A) of title 42section 5106a(b)(2)(B) of title 42Pub. L. 111–320, title I, § 115(c)(2)(A)124 Stat. 3469Dec. 20, 2010

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The Secretary of Health and Human Services, in consultation with the Attorney General, shall create a national registry of substantiated cases of child abuse or neglect.

children.heading: Collection
children.label: 1 Collection
children.text: The information in the registry described in subsection (a) shall be supplied by States and Indian tribes, or, at the option of a State, by political subdivisions of such State, to the Secretary of Health and Human Services.

children.heading: Type of information
children.label: 2 Type of information
children.text: The registry described in subsection (a) shall collect in a central electronic registry information on persons reported to a State, Indian tribe, or political subdivision of a State as perpetrators of a substantiated case of child abuse or neglect.

children.heading: Treatment of reports
children.label: A Treatment of reports
children.text: The information to be provided to the Secretary of Health and Human Services under this section shall relate to substantiated reports of child abuse or neglect.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: B Exception
children.text: If a State, Indian tribe, or political subdivision of a State has an electronic register of cases of child abuse or neglect equivalent to the registry established under this section that it maintains pursuant to a requirement or authorization under any other provision of law, the information provided to the Secretary of Health and Human Services under this section shall be coextensive with that in such register.

children.label: A
children.text: shall be in a standardized electronic form determined by the Secretary of Health and Human Services; and

children.label: B
children.text: shall contain case-specific identifying information that is limited to the name of the perpetrator and the nature of the substantiated case of child abuse or neglect, and that complies with clauses (viii) and (ix) of section 5106a(b)(2)(A) of title 42.1See References in Text note below.1
an equivalent register of cases of child abuse or neglect that it maintains pursuant to a requirement or authorization under any other provision of law; or
any other record relating to child abuse or neglect, regardless of whether the report of abuse or neglect was substantiated, unsubstantiated, or determined to be unfounded.

children.heading: Accessibility
children.label: e Accessibility
children.text: Information contained in the national registry shall only be accessible to any Federal, State, Indian tribe, or local government entity, or any agent of such entities, that has a need for such information in order to carry out its responsibilities under law to protect children from child abuse and neglect.

children.heading: Dissemination
children.label: f Dissemination
children.text: The Secretary of Health and Human Services shall establish standards for the dissemination of information in the national registry of substantiated cases of child abuse or neglect. Such standards shall comply with clauses (viii) and (ix) of section 5106a(b)(2)(A) of title 42.1

children.label: A
children.text: costs and benefits of such data collection standards;

children.label: B
children.text: data collection standards currently employed by each State, Indian tribe, or political subdivision of a State;

children.label: C
children.text: data collection standards that should be considered to establish a model of promising practices; and

children.label: D
children.text: a due process procedure for a national registry.

children.heading: Report
children.label: 2 Report
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , the Secretary of Homeland Security shall submit to the Committees on the Judiciary in the House of Representatives and the United States Senate and the Senate Committee on Health, Education, Labor and Pensions and the House Committee on Education and the Workforce a report containing the recommendations and findings of the study on data collection standards for a national child abuse registry authorized under this subsection.July 27, 2006

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 3 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated $500,000 for the period of fiscal years 2006 and 2007 to carry out the study required by this subsection.
